meta.scene_summary: We are busy inventing new ways to break the internet while the old ways are still catching fire.

cooking.headline: Local residents are treating datacenters like unwanted roommates
cooking.summary: Communities are literally recalling local officials for letting massive datacenters move into their backyards. It turns out people care less about the future of AI and more about their neighborhood sounding like a runway at takeoff.
cooking.plain_english: US residents are starting recall elections for local politicians who approved the construction of large data centers in their communities.

cooking.headline: Starlink is solving the 'no internet' problem by literally bypassing the planet's infrastructure.
cooking.summary: Africans are increasingly turning to Starlink to bypass unreliable local infrastructure that treats uptime as a suggestion. It turns out the most effective way to fix a legacy network is to just launch your own satellites into orbit.
cooking.plain_english: People in Africa are increasingly using Starlink satellite internet to access reliable connectivity in areas where traditional ground-based networks are unavailable or inconsistent.

cooking.headline: The best way to optimize code is to turn it into a picture and squint at it
cooking.summary: Engineers discovered that converting code into images for an OCR model to read cuts costs by 60%. It turns out the fastest way to run your codebase is to treat it like a bad handwritten note from a doctor.
cooking.plain_english: A company reduced its software operating costs by converting code into images and using character recognition software to process the information.

cooking.headline: Finally, a Wireshark for the AI agents that keep talking behind your back
cooking.summary: Mcpsnoop lets you watch the traffic between your AI agents in real-time, essentially turning your model's private conversations into a public spectacle. It's like being the fly on the wall in a group project meeting where nobody is actually doing the work.
cooking.plain_english: A new tool allows developers to monitor and view the communications between AI models and their connected tools in real-time.

cooked.headline: Claude Mythos launch is speedrunning a security disaster
cooked.summary: Anthropic just dropped a new model, and the security vulnerabilities are spiking faster than a junior dev's first git push to production. It's the digital equivalent of shipping a house with the front door wide open and a 'welcome' mat for hackers.
cooked.plain_english: The release of the new Claude Mythos AI model has coincided with a significant increase in reported security vulnerabilities.

cooked.headline: Rust Coreutils just proved that 'rewritten in Rust' is still just 'rewritten with new bugs'.
cooked.summary: The community attempted to replace standard Linux utilities with Rust-based versions, only to break Ubuntu builds because the new tools didn't copy files quite like the originals. It's the classic developer trap of assuming a rewrite is the same as an upgrade.
cooked.plain_english: A project to replace standard Linux system tools with versions written in the Rust programming language caused issues with Ubuntu software builds due to unexpected behavioral differences.

cooked.headline: MSI Center's security is basically an open door with a 'welcome' mat
cooked.summary: MSI designed their software to give local users system-level privileges with almost no effort required. It turns out that 'ease of use' for the end user also makes life incredibly easy for any local malware looking to take over your PC.
cooked.plain_english: A vulnerability in MSI software allows a standard computer user to gain administrative control over the entire operating system.

cooked.headline: The irony of being hacked while investigating spyware is peak tech
cooked.summary: European officials were busy investigating the use of invasive spyware when they found their own phones had been compromised by the exact same tech. It is the digital equivalent of a fire inspector's office burning down because they forgot to check their own smoke detectors.
cooked.plain_english: Members of the European Parliament investigating the use of unauthorized spyware were themselves targeted and infected by spyware.

cooked.headline: AI coding is officially the new doomscrolling
cooked.summary: Engineers are getting hooked on the high of AI-generated code while the actual complexity of their codebase stays exactly the same. It turns out that 'automating the boring stuff' is just a fancy way of saying you're now addicted to a chatbot that writes your technical debt for you.
cooked.plain_english: Software engineers are developing a dependency on AI tools that may be negatively impacting their overall productivity and well-being.

cooked.headline: Your 3% productivity boost is basically just a rounding error
cooked.summary: Companies are betting their entire budgets on AI efficiency gains that barely cover the cost of a fancy lunch. It turns out that saving three percent of your time is just enough to create a new meeting to discuss why the AI isn't doing the other ninety-seven percent.
cooked.plain_english: Research indicates that AI tools provide only a marginal increase in worker productivity that does not translate into significant cost savings for businesses.
